title: Seeing Manimekalai initiates pursuit

title: Pursuit continues despite renunciant direction and refusal

title: Royal rank multiplies private desire

title: Sincerity does not create consent

title: Previous-life connection creates no present claim

title: Altered identity creates asymmetric knowledge
claim_or_observation: Manimekalai's altered form changes recognition while Udayakumaran and Kanchanan act with incomplete and unequal information

title: Kanchanan kills through mistaken perception

title: Death is a multi-agent causal tragedy
claim_or_observation: Continued pursuit disguise social relationships mistaken interpretation and lethal action interact without making one person the sole cause

title: Death is not deserved punishment or romantic sacrifice
claim_or_observation: The fatal scene must not celebrate violence as punishment for pursuit or beautify death as proof of pure love

title: The prince's death drives palace grief and coercion
claim_or_observation: Udayakumaran's death becomes the basis of Rajamadevi's grief and later misuse of palace power against Manimekalai

title: Charisma and unrealised public capacity deepen tragedy
claim_or_observation: Showing credible public promise makes his failure of restraint politically consequential without excusing pursuit
